title: How to Read Errors & Get Unstuck
story_hook: That red screen that made you quit last time? Today it becomes your best friend. Tier-4 students who learn this never drop out again.

content.story: That red screen that made you quit last time? Today it becomes your best friend. Tier-4 students who learn this never drop out again.

content.title: Finding and Fixing Code Errors for Beginners
content.focus_note: Watch how the error message is actually helping you.

content.doc_md:
Error = computer's love letter.

Google the exact message + 'Python/Java'.

15-minute rule: try 15 min, then ask.

visualizer.task: Paste sample buggy code and see the line highlighted.
visualizer.notes: Focus on reading the line number and message.

quiz_questions.question: What part of an error tells you the exact line to check?
options: The file name and line number
quiz_questions.correctAnswer: The file name and line number
quiz_questions.explanation: Line numbers point you to the precise location of the issue.

quiz_questions.question: What should you Google when stuck?
quiz_questions.explanation: Searching the exact error message gives the most relevant results.

quiz_questions.question: What is the 15-minute rule?
options: Try for 15 minutes, then ask for help
Only code for 15 minutes a day
quiz_questions.correctAnswer: Try for 15 minutes, then ask for help
quiz_questions.explanation: You learn by trying first, then asking with clear context.

content.task_prompt: Debug this 5-line Maggi recipe code that has 2 errors.

content.connection_map: Errors happen when your algorithm step is wrong.
recall_questions: What does an error message usually tell you first?
Why is Googling an error not cheating?
content.identity_affirmation: You are the kind of coder who reads errors calmly and keeps moving.
